title: Computed styles and layout inspectors

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Computed styles and layout inspectors; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: MDN CSS Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Computed styles and layout inspectors
sources.claim: CSS debugging compares matched rules, cascade priority, computed values, box geometry, layout overlays, and responsive states before editing.

canonicalExplanation.summary: CSS debugging compares matched rules, cascade priority, computed values, box geometry, layout overlays, and responsive states before editing.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A learner finds overflow by inspecting the grid track minimum rather than adding random widths. Your goal is to diagnose a cascade and a layout failure using recorded developer-tool evidence. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Responsive design and motion; Stylesheet organization; Inheritance, initial and computed values; Flexbox and Grid. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: CSS debugging compares matched rules, cascade priority, computed values, box geometry, layout overlays, and responsive states before editing. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: DevTools can show a simulated state that differs from real input, and local edits disappear unless transferred to source. This draft therefore rejects: The Styles panel shows only declarations that currently win, so crossed-out rules can be ignored.

includes: Use matched rules, computed values, box, flex and grid inspectors to find the actual cause of a defect.
Diagnose a cascade and a layout failure using recorded developer-tool evidence.
excludes: DevTools can show a simulated state that differs from real input, and local edits disappear unless transferred to source.

outcomes: Diagnose a cascade and a layout failure using recorded developer-tool evidence.

examples.title: Computed styles and layout inspectors: concrete case
examples.situation: A learner finds overflow by inspecting the grid track minimum rather than adding random widths.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A learner finds overflow by inspecting the grid track minimum rather than adding random widths.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Inspect matched and overridden rules plus computed values; a crossed-out declaration often explains the competing cascade.
examples.result: The result follows because Inspect matched and overridden rules plus computed values; a crossed-out declaration often explains the competing cascade.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Computed styles and layout inspectors, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Computed styles and layout inspectors before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
